mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 2.98% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS above 150,000 persons triggers positive 5-day forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength and construction activity. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to improved earnings visibility and economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 380,197 million dollars, HOUST at 1,012 thousand units
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on RSAFS and HOUST growth indicators, or joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and future earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO declines are positive in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, equity valuations compress due to earnings recession expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock event
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.5%, the Fed is behind the inflation curve, creating policy uncertainty that depresses equity valuations over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate directly reflects aggregate demand strength and corporate earnings capacity. Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11,677.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-2.5% GDPC1 growth quarters are negative in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while PAYEMS growth remains above 100,000 persons, labor hoarding signals imminent production cuts and negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 114.2334, PAYEMS at 137,993 thousand
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.
